text: Welcome to FS 572 Fermentation Science & Technology! The syllabus is posted in Materials. Please review the lab safety guide and complete the intro survey before our first lab on Thursday.
Reminder: Problem Set 2 on enzyme kinetics is due Friday. Review the mash chemistry slides and bring questions to office hours Thursday 3:30-5:00 in Babcock Hall.
Guest lecture next Tuesday: Dr. Patricia Liang will discuss bacterial cultures in fermented dairy. Attendance counts toward participation. Bring your reading notes.
No lecture Thursday - the department food-science symposium runs all afternoon. Use the time to start your yeast propagation lab report.
Welcome to the FS 572 Lab! Before our first session make sure you have closed-toe shoes a lab notebook and have read the sanitation SOP. We start with aseptic technique.
Great work on the kimchi and sauerkraut ferments everyone. I posted a few standout lab reports in Materials as examples of clear methods and good data tables.
Reminder: Koji incubation lab report due Wednesday. Make sure your temperature and humidity logs are complete. See me if your koji did not bloom as expected.
Welcome to the FS 572 seminar! This section is about reading critically and tasting carefully - come ready to discuss the Fermentation journal articles and evaluate samples.
Sensory analysis project presentations start next week. Sign up for your slot on the shared Google Doc and confirm which fermented product you will evaluate.
Term paper on a fermented product is due to the dropbox by Dec 12. Final exam is Dec 18 covering all units. No extensions on the term paper - plan ahead.

Distillation module begins May 7 in Babcock Hall 205 starting at 0800. Each team will run the pot still on the fermented corn-and-rice mash. Pre-stage your sanitized glassware and have your safety checklist ready.

Pre-distillation prep checklist posted in Materials. Verify still gaskets clean condenser water lines and stage your collection jars by cut. Your lab partner will log temperature and ABV from the parrot.

Distillation runs reviewed. Result 13 PASS / 5 needs-rework out of 18 station checks. Key issues - Station 8 flare-off temperature ran high / Station 4 heads cut collected late / Station 2 condenser water too warm / Station 14 proofing math error / Station 17 sanitation log incomplete. Re-shoot daylight photos for Station 11 and Station 18.

Prioritized fixes for the next distillation session. P0 - Station 8 recalibrate thermometer / Station 4 re-run heads cut / Station 14 redo proofing calculation. P1 - Station 2 chill condenser water / Station 17 complete sanitation log. P2 - re-photograph Station 11 and Station 18 setups in daylight. See mat_010 for the protocol reference.
Prior-year distillation module ran May 9 in Babcock Hall 205 starting 0800. All teams ran the pot still on the fermented mash and staged glassware for review.

Prior-year distillation module complete. Result all 18 station checks PASS. Notes for next year - Station 8 thermometer drift worth re-checking / Station 14 proofing math needs a second reviewer / Station 2 condenser water trended warm. Address before the 2026 session.
